ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ROBERT HALF INTERNATIONAL INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. RHI PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SATISFY ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION ON MR. ROGERS' FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION FOR RACE AND AGE DISCRIMINATION. MR. ROGERS' REQUEST TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT IS DENIED BECAUSE MR. ROGERS DOES NOT EXPLAIN WHAT AMENDMENTS HE SEEKS TO MAKE AND HOW THOSE AMENDMENTS WOULD REMEDY THE DEFICIENCIES THAT LED TO THE GRANTING OF THIS MOTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR #8189 =302/HK

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication And Notice Of Motion
Off Calendar Apr-21-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - BRYAN ROGERS, TEL. NO. 1-510-783-1673, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. TRACI BERNARD-MARKS OF NIXON PEABODY LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-984-8208 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Robert Half International Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. RHI presented sufficient evidence to satisfy its burden of production on Mr. Rogers' first and second causes of action for race and age discrimination. RHI's Facts 9-11 and14 and paragraph 7 of the Doran Ingalls declaration show that Mr. Rogers' failure to provide the requested references and an updated resume was the reason why RHI did not consider Mr. Rogers for a position. In opposition, Mr. Rogers merely takes issue with RHI's business practices. Mr. Rogers complains that his resume was accurate, RHI already had references on file, and RHI had no formal policy with regard to references. To survive summary judgment, however, Mr. Rogers must do more than show that RHI acted inconsistently or unfairly. Mr. Rogers must present substantial responsive evidence showing that racial or age animus was a motivating factor for RHI's conduct. (Hersant v. Dept. of Social Services (1997) 57 Cal.App.4th 997, 1003; Green v. Rancho Santa Margarita Mortgage Co. (1994) 28 Cal.App.4th 686, 695; Hicks v. KNTV Television, Inc. (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 994, 1003.) Mr. Rogers has not presented any evidence of racial or ageist animus by RHI. Mr. Rogers cannot establish the failure to prevent discrimination cause of action because there is no underlying violation of FEHA. (Trujillo v. North County Transit Dist. (1998) 63 Cal.App.4th 280, 288-289.) Mr. Rogers cannot establish the discrimination in violation of public policy cause of action. Mr. Rogers cites no authority showing that such a claim applies where there was no termination of employment. Even assuming such a claim exists, the absence of any evidence of any racial or ageist animus by RHI precludes the successful assertion of such a claim in this case. Mr. Rogers' request to amend the complaint is denied because Mr. Rogers does not explain what amendments he seeks to make and how those amendments would remedy the deficiencies that led to the granting of this motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
